methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $130M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $500M - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 22.3x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Positron AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series B), post-money $1B+

data.narrative:
MatX is a Series B semiconductor company founded by Reiner Pope that has raised $130M to date. The valuation analysis employs a peer set of 8 private and 3 public companies in the chips and semiconductors sector, including stage-adjacent comparables such as Positron AI (Series B, $1.0B), Etched (Series A, $5.0B), d-Matrix (Series A, $2.0B), and public anchors including NVDA, AMD, and ALAB.

Two quantitative methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.9x multiplier to the $130M total raised proxy, implying a valuation of $247M. Peer-median anchoring across six same-sector, same/adjacent-stage companies yields a median valuation of $1.0B, with a range spanning $500M to $5.0B. Public-comp P/S anchoring (semiconductors sector median of 22.3x across the three public tickers) cannot be applied due to the absence of MatX's revenue figures on file. These two methods triangulate to a low of $210M, median of $624M, and high of $1.1B.

The wide dispersion between stage-progression and peer-median methods reflects uncertainty regarding MatX's relative technology maturity and capital efficiency versus its peers. The peer set itself spans significant valuation dispersion, particularly among Series A entrants. Resolution of valuation range will likely depend on product-market fit evidence, customer traction, and burn profile relative to the peer cohort.
